76428.subject: ⚔️ Kimi vs the US Treasury
76428.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://utm.genai.works/r/he2t9cz)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** The White House accused China's Moonshot of stealing Anthropic's Fable to build Kimi K3 and floated sanctions, the whole industry thinks Claude Opus 5 ships today, OpenAI is teasing a drop everyone's calling GPT-6 and Google grew revenue 24% while burning $5.9 billion in cash on AI.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **Moonshot:** Did China really steal Claude?

* **Anthropic:** Is Claude Opus 5 dropping today?

* **OpenAI:** What is GPT-6 rumored to do?

* **Google:** How did record revenue still spook Wall Street?


----------

----------
## [The US says China stole Claude to build Kimi](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/22/treasury-threatens-sanctions-after-white-house-claims-moonshot-distilled-anthropics-fable/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/24d85486-b5c6-45ca-be19-2cf114057f2e/Beehiiv_01_-_Jul_24.png?t=1784884008)
Caption: 

The White House accused a Chinese lab of stealing Anthropic's best model, and Treasury is dangling sanctions over it.

On Tuesday the White House science chief, Michael Kratsios, said Moonshot ran a large-scale distillation attack on Anthropic's Fable to build Kimi K3, the open model that sold out this month.

Distillation means training your model on another model's answers. It's common and often legal. Doing it at industrial scale on a rival's crown jewel is the part Washington is calling theft.

Treasury's Scott Bessent said open source is not open season on American IP, and put sanctions and a spot on the trade blacklist on the table.

The timeline doesn't add up. Fable only went public on July 1 and Kimi K3 shipped on the 15th.

Researchers say two weeks is nowhere near enough to copy a frontier model and then beat it, which K3 does on some coding tests.

Either Moonshot is faster than America wants to admit, or the case is thinner than the threat.


----------

----------
## [Learn the language every AI is built on](https://utm.genai.works/r/w5jfa25)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/39e0c675-d9f2-49f7-9df9-4b26f81c50ca/16.png?t=1784885606)
Follow image link: (https://academy.genai.works/courses/python-programming-mastery/details?utm_source=beehiiv&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=academy_promotion_2026&utm_content=beehiiv_newsletter_python_jul&utm_term=w5jfa25)
Caption: 

Python runs the AI tools you use every day. If you want to build with AI instead of only using it, this is where you start.

76427.subject: DeepSeek's leaked investor call: inside the AI Playbook that erased $589 billion of Nvidia's value 📉
76427.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/deepseeks-leaked-investor-call

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 397k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
Liang Wenfeng has given roughly two interviews in his life. So when a 3-hour-44-minute recording of DeepSeek’s founder walking his new shareholders through the entire playbook (pricing math, GPU counts, the Huawei bet, a step-by-step AGI roadmap) leaked out of a May 20, 2026 closed-door meeting, it instantly became the most complete picture available of the AI lab that erased $589 billion of Nvidia’s $NVDA  market value in a single trading day in January 2025.
The meeting was Liang’s debrief for the incoming investors in DeepSeek’s first external financing ever: roughly ¥50 billion (~$7.4 billion) at a post-money valuation of about $52 billion, per a regulatory filing surfaced by Caixin, from Tencent, CATL, JD.com and NetEase, with Liang personally putting in around ¥20 billion (~$3 billion) of his own money. Within weeks, a follow-on round was reportedly in early talks at around $70 billion 😳
Before we dive in, a word on sourcing. Everything below comes from a reconciled transcript, an English transcription of the audio, checked against the full Chinese text. The figures are Liang’s own claims, and DeepSeek itself has (unsurprisingly) not confirmed the document’s authenticity. But the details keep checking out. The most telling cross-check: his pricing rule of a ten-month hardware payback, roughly sixfold profit on compute cost, lines up with the 545% theoretical inference margin DeepSeek itself disclosed publicly in March 2025. Names of some competitor models are garbled by speech recognition and are flagged where relevant.
The call breaks down into six stories: the sixfold pricing rule that turns open weights into a moat, the first on-the-record GPU numbers from any Chinese frontier lab, a one-year deadline for breaking NVIDIA’s CUDA lock-in, the org design that has half of DeepSeek’s core researchers labeling data, a dated watchlist of falsifiable claims, and a ranked map of where the opportunity moves next. More importantly, the stakes reach well past DeepSeek, since the same pricing doctrine sets the terms of competition for GLM-5.2 [ https://substack.com/redirect/30f4bebd-2aaf-48b1-8f8a-e41f6dbec831?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], Kimi K3 [ https://substack.com/redirect/e9ffa71d-49a9-4597-8c3b-9a214a09f5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and the wider Chinese open-weight field.
If you build, operate, or invest anywhere near AI, the rest of this piece will pay itself back faster than Liang’s GPUs.
1. Why this meeting happened at all...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2F

CMC Market Pulse:
The Essential Weekly Crypto Briefing

Welcome to this week’s CMC Market Pulse newsletter!

CMC Market Pulse delivers actionable market insights, giving you
all the key narratives and developments you need in under 5
minutes.

Let's dive into this week.

Market Overview 📈
-----------------

Bitcoin (BTC) gained strength again this week, climbing
4.06%
this week. Ethereum (ETH) trailed close behind, finishing the
week up
3.53%.
Total crypto market cap rose again, rising to
$2.22T,
up 2% from $2.17T in the week before.

Weekly liquidations continue to stay contained, with this week’s
pattern mirroring the prior week. Short liquidations rose early
in the week, with sentiment turned against the longs closing to
the end of the week. Funding rates largely remained unchanged
across the week, mostly remaining in healthy positive or slight
negative values.

* Michael Saylor’s Strategy increases their USD reserve by
another $225M through share sales, bringing their USD reserve to
$3.2B.
* S&P and Pantera collaborate to release the S&P Pantera Digital
Asset Index, an index focused on productive assets and
fundamentals in the crypto space.
* Tokenized stocks provider, xStocks, expands from U.S. equities
to include Hong Kong equities, supported by GTN Global.
The crypto market pushes higher, in spite of the weakness in
equities triggered by AI capex fears and the escalating conflict
in Iran
